title: What to know about dry cough before using medicinal herbs
intro: Several herbal teas efficiently soothe dry coughs. Licorice root tea, with its natural demulcent properties, coats and soothes irritated throat tissues. Thyme tea, containing antispasmodic compounds, helps relax the muscles in the trachea and reduces coughing fits. Lastly, slippery elm tea, rich in mucilage, creates a protective barrier over the respiratory tract, alleviating irritation that triggers a dry cough. These herbal teas serve as natural, reliable remedies for combating dry cough symptoms.
definition: A dry cough is a type of cough that does not produce any mucus or phlegm. It is caused by irritation in the throat and upper chest. Dry cough differs from a productive cough, which brings up mucus from the lungs. Dry cough is important because it can interfere with sleep, cause difficulty speaking, and even lead to exhaustion, particularly in children and the elderly. Furthermore, a persistent dry cough can indicate underlying health issues, such as asthma, allergies, or respiratory infections, and may require medical attention.
